purpose: Baseline manifest for slow analysis-depth upgrades. Synthetic fixtures come first; real-binary assertions remain guarded until stable artifacts and expected outputs are recorded.

trustBoundary.aetherframeForge: optional advisory packaging/refinement only when policy permits
trustBoundary.analysisSurfaces: disassembly, CFG, TALON, STRIKE, decompiler, debugger state, and traces are analyst evidence/advisory surfaces

syntheticFixtures.notes: Uses hand-authored instruction arrays to avoid fragile real-binary output in the first foundation slice.
Proves uncertain boundaries become warnings instead of exact claims.
Ensures the new model does not include classification or threat-score fields.

plannedFixtures.guard: Only run when nest_cli and selected local samples exist; do not make release or correctness claims from historical outputs.

notes: The first exit goal is a typed foundation, not better decompiler output.
Pseudo-C remains analyst guidance, not recovered source.
Debugger and STRIKE live/runtime behavior are documented as current baseline only and are not expanded in this slice.
